message:
Add min-age pre-filtering to agent plan/apply and enrich summary metadata

Agent plan changes:
- Apply --min-age filter before sampling rather than after, so the sample
  is drawn only from processes that have been alive long enough. Previously,
  short-lived processes consumed sample slots and were then post-filtered,
  reducing effective sample size.
- Track candidates_evaluated as a separate variable reflecting post-filter
  count, fixing the summary JSON which previously reported the unfiltered
  filter_result.passed.len()
- Include min_age, sample_size, include_kernel_threads, and deep flags in
  the summary metadata for reproducibility

Agent apply changes:
- Implement --min-age guard at apply time: re-scans target PIDs via
  quick_scan and drops any whose elapsed time is below the threshold,
  preventing action on recently-spawned processes that inherited a stale PID

E2E test update:
- Add --min-age 315360000 (10 years) to the sampling scenario so the test
  reliably produces zero candidates on any system

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -251,8 +251,16 @@ mod e2e_scenarios {
 
         // Use extremely high min-age to ensure no candidates
         // Note: We can't guarantee no candidates on a busy system, so we accept both exit codes
-        pt_core()
-            .args(["--format", "json", "agent", "plan", "--sample-size", "10"])
+        pt_core().args([
+            "--format",
+            "json",
+            "agent",
+            "plan",
+            "--sample-size",
+            "10",
+            "--min-age",
+            "315360000",
+        ])
             .assert()
             .code(predicate::in_iter([0, 1])); // 0 = no candidates, 1 = candidates found
